column1: #Text=The hooded crow, also colloquially called just hoodie, is a Eurasian bird subspecies of the carrion crow in the genus Corvus.
#Text=Widely distributed, it is found across Northern, Eastern, and Southeastern Europe, as well as parts of the Middle East.
#Text=It is an ashy-grey bird with black head, throat, wings, tail, and thigh feathers, as well as a black bill, eyes, and feet.
#Text=Like other corvids, it is an omnivorous and opportunistic forager and feeder.
